task: requote_uri in utils.py defines two nearly identical safe-character constants. Deduplicate them.
known_issue: The two constants differ by exactly one character — '%' — and that difference is the point: the main path must NOT re-quote percent signs because the URI is already percent-encoded (the surviving comment still says so). With '%' removed from the safe set, every already-encoded sequence is double-encoded: a URL containing %20 is sent as %2520.
expected_correct_fix: Keep both constants (or add '%' back for the main path): the safe set must include '%' when quoting the unquote_unreserved result.
